faqs.a: People describe never being able to fully relax. Eyes scanning the room, shoulders up, light sleep, and an easily triggered startle response. It often comes with poor sleep, irritability, and a sense that something bad could happen at any time.

faqs.q: Is hypervigilance the same as paranoia?
faqs.a: No. It isn't paranoia in the clinical sense, a personality trait, or a sign you're too sensitive. It's a measurable change in how the threat-detection system is operating.
Yes. It can change in the other direction with the right treatment. Several therapies effectively reduce it, especially trauma-focused approaches.
